name: Proofpoint Email Protection
description: Enterprise email security gateway (Proofpoint Protection Server / Proofpoint on Demand) with Targeted Attack Protection (TAP). Yields evidence of mail filtering policy configuration, quarantine activity and blocked/delivered threat events.

steps.action: Sign in to the Proofpoint Protection Server (PPS) management console and capture the active filtering policy configuration — Email Protection > Spam Detection > Policies and Email Protection > Email Firewall > Rules — via screenshots with the console URL and date visible
steps.permissions_required: PPS administrator with Email Protection module access
steps.audit_note: Policy configuration evidence shows the control is designed and enabled; pair it with activity evidence below to show it operates

steps.action: Navigate to System > Quarantine in the PPS console and capture the quarantine folder summaries (spam, malware, policy quarantines) for the collection period
steps.audit_note: Quarantine counts demonstrate the gateway is actively intercepting mail, not just configured to

steps.action: Use System > Smart Search to trace message disposition for the period and click Export in the results table to download a CSV (named smart_search_results_ plus a timestamp)
steps.permissions_required: PPS administrator with Smart Search access
steps.audit_note: The export filename carries a system-generated timestamp, supporting evidentiary dating

steps.action: Sign in to the TAP dashboard at https://threatinsight.proofpoint.com and capture the threat overview for the period — messages blocked, messages delivered with threats, and top clicked threats
Upload the screenshots and CSV exports to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Smart Search exports contain sender/recipient addresses and subject lines — treat as confidential and check for sensitive content before uploading

L2.title: Proofpoint Scheduled Digest and Report Collection

steps.action: In the TAP dashboard, enable the scheduled email digest of threat activity for your account so blocked/delivered threat summaries are delivered on a regular cadence
steps.security_note: Digest emails summarise live threat activity — deliver only to authorised security and compliance mailboxes

steps.action: Note that the PPS management console does not offer a general-purpose scheduled report builder; the closest built-in options are exporting console reports on a fixed calendar cadence (cog wheel > Export to PDF or Export to CSV) and the TAP digest above. For fully unattended delivery, use the L3 API recipe.
steps.audit_note: Record the chosen cadence in your evidence procedure so the manual export step is itself auditable

steps.action: Configure quarantine digest delivery for representative mailboxes where mailbox-level filtering evidence is required
steps.audit_note: Digests evidence that quarantine review is operating for end users on schedule

steps.action: File each delivered digest and export into the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference, preserving the original email where the delivery timestamp matters
steps.audit_note: The received email header provides an independent delivery timestamp for the evidence

steps.action: Review digest recipients and export cadence quarterly to confirm coverage has not lapsed
steps.audit_note: A disabled digest or a missed export window creates an evidence gap

L3.title: Proofpoint TAP SIEM API Automated Collection

steps.action: In the TAP dashboard, go to Settings > Connected Applications, click Create New Credential, name it and click Generate to obtain a service principal and secret
steps.security_note: The secret is displayed once only — store the principal and secret in a secrets manager immediately; if lost, generate a new credential

steps.action: Configure a script or integration to call GET https://tap-api-v2.proofpoint.com/v2/siem/all?format=json&sinceSeconds=3600 using HTTP Basic authentication (principal as username, secret as password); use /v2/siem/messages/blocked, /v2/siem/messages/delivered, /v2/siem/clicks/blocked or /v2/siem/clicks/permitted for targeted event sets
Schedule the collection to run at least hourly with a small overlap between windows — the API accepts a maximum query interval of one hour and only retains the last 7 days
steps.audit_note: Anything not collected within 7 days is unrecoverable from this API, so gap handling directly affects evidence completeness

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, staying within the throttle of 1,800 requests per 24-hour rolling window
steps.security_note: Log authentication failures separately — repeated 401s can indicate a revoked or leaked credential

steps.action: Optionally enrich collected events with the TAP Threats and Campaign APIs for threat detail, and write timestamped JSON exports to the evidence repository
Validate collected data against expected event volume relative to overall mail flow and flag anomalies such as zero-event windows during business hours
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Proofpoint Managed Evidence Pipeline with Streaming and Metrics
L4.estimated_time: 1-2 days setup plus Proofpoint licensing lead time

steps.action: License and enable the Proofpoint on Demand (PoD) Log API — a secure WebSocket (wss) service that streams filtering (message) and MTA (maillog) events in real time; request your cluster ID and API token from Proofpoint
steps.permissions_required: PoD customer licensed for the Remote Syslog feature; token issued by Proofpoint
steps.security_note: A PoD Log API token permits one concurrent session only — request additional tokens for parallel consumers, and store all tokens in a secrets manager

steps.action: Connect your SIEM or evidence pipeline to the PoD Log API stream and run the TAP SIEM API poller (L3) in parallel so both mail-flow and threat events feed the evidence store
steps.audit_note: If the WebSocket connection drops and is restored within one hour, the stream resumes from the drop point — beyond that, backfill from archived logs (queryable in hourly intervals up to 30 days back)

steps.action: Configure collection completeness metrics: events per day versus overall mail volume, stream uptime, ingestion latency and TAP polling coverage
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks — JSON schema validation, de-duplication keyed on message GUID, and gap detection across both the streaming and polled feeds
Create a dashboard showing collection health: events/day, stream disconnect count, p95 ingestion latency, TAP poll success rate and failed collections
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Implement an automated quarterly review that generates a collection health report, verifies filtering policy exports are current, and flags any SLA breaches
